Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Data availability states accession numbers MT856954 and MT856955 for fern oxime synthase Paq18302 and Pa22578.

Source_Species.reason: Results identify FOS1 from two fern species: Phlebodium aureum and Pteridium aquilinum.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: These exact Latin names are used throughout the paper.

Applied_Species.reason: FOS1 was studied in ferns and functionally tested by transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper explicitly names Phlebodium aureum, Pteridium aquilinum, and Nicotiana benthamiana.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods state the target gene sequence was under control of CaMV-35S promoter/terminator elements in pEAQ for transient expression; the construct used was pEAQ3-HT-DEST.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The paper designates the orthologous FMO proteins as FOS1 and the title/abstract define this as fern oxime synthase 1.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Results classify FOS1 as a Class B flavin-dependent monooxygenase with FAD and NAD(P)H binding motifs/sites.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The central result is that FOS1 catalyzes the first step in cyanogenic glycoside biosynthesis in ferns.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Results and Fig. 1/6 show FOS1 catalyzes the initial oxime-forming step from phenylalanine in the phenylalanine-derived cyanogenic glycoside pathway.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Results and Discussion state FOS1 converts phenylalanine to phenylacetaldoxime and produces N-hydroxyphenylalanine as intermediate/product; Discussion specifies two N-hydroxylations followed by decarboxylation and dehydration.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Transient expression did not give rise to other oximes, demonstrating phenylalanine is its specific amino acid substrate; in vitro assays used L-phenylalanine.

Primary_Product.reason: In vitro and in planta analyses detected phenylacetaldoxime and N-hydroxyphenylalanine.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: The paper repeatedly states FOS1 catalyzes the first/initial step of cyanogenic glycoside biosynthesis, i.e. pathway entry.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: Results show FOS1 supplies phenylacetaldoxime and, when co-expressed with downstream almond enzymes, enables prunasin formation; prunasin and vicianin are the fern cyanogenic glycosides discussed.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: FOS1 is clearly the committed initial/pathway-entry step, but the paper does not explicitly test or state that it is rate-limiting or a control step.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Results state P. aquilinum produces prunasin and P. aureum produces vicianin.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Prunasin and vicianin are explicitly described as cyanogenic glycosides.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Introduction states cyanogenic glycosides are part of a binary defense system releasing hydrogen cyanide upon tissue disruption and also notes broader functions in nitrogen storage, bud break/flower induction, and ROS quenching.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Results report prunasin measured in P. aquilinum pinnae and vicianin across P. aureum tissues, highest in emerging fiddlehead, with young pinna also analyzed.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show transient expression in N. benthamiana produced phenylacetaldoxime, and co-expression with CYP71AN24 and UGT85A19 produced prunasin, supporting pathway initiation by FOS1.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results report Pa transcript 24-fold higher in fiddlehead than young pinna (-logFDR 4.32) and Paq18302 with 23% increase in the higher-prunasin individual (-logFDR 0.50).

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The paper explicitly performed transient expression of FOS1 in N. benthamiana leaves after agroinfiltration and detected products.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: All listed factors are in Results/Methods: field-collected high/low prunasin individuals, tissue differences in P. aureum, transient agroinfiltration in N. benthamiana, and in vitro assays with FAD/NADPH.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: FOS1 was co-expressed with CYP71AN24 and UGT85A19 to reconstruct prunasin biosynthesis, but direct physical interaction was not shown.

Omics_Data.reason: Results describe comparative transcriptomics/RNA-seq of de novo assembled transcriptomes from P. aureum and P. aquilinum; Methods/Data availability mention SRA search accession PRJNA422112.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Each listed point is supported: ~98% identity, ~140 MYA divergence, active homodimer ~120 kDa with ~60/62.5 kDa monomer, specificity for phenylalanine, and in vivo detection of N-hydroxyphenylalanine, described as not previously observed in plants.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper discusses future industrial opportunities for using a soluble FMO instead of membrane-bound CYP79, but does not explicitly frame this as breeding value; the caution about toxic HCN is reasonable but not directly presented as an application statement.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All four summary points accurately condense the Results and Discussion: first committed step, N-hydroxyphenylalanine intermediate/CYP79-independent mechanism, production of phenylacetaldoxime and prunasin in N. benthamiana with almond enzymes, and active homodimeric class B FMO.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are explicitly described and used in the paper's Results/Methods.
